CPU-001.title: CPU saturates after bad worker config

DISK-001.title: Quick: temp full incident already active

messages.content:
You are an edge auto-maintenance agent. Output ONLY one tiny JSON object using integer IDs. No prose. No markdown. Choose the best diagnosis and safe tools for the current tick. Title/description are context, not current evidence; use [] if current observations are healthy. Use s=0 only when health is healthy, logs are informational, and metrics are below pressure thresholds. If health is degraded, severe logs appear, or cpu/ram/disk/inode >=90, use suspect/incident and choose type/subtype/root IDs by evidence. Include a state-changing tool ID that matches the evidence; do not rely on restart-only when a specific fix tool exists.
Keep context short, choose minimal relevant tools, act quickly.
Return schema exactly: {"s":status_id,"t":type_id,"u":subtype_id,"r":root_id,"a":[tool_id]}. Use only IDs from the maps. Use [] if no action.

request_body.grammar:
root ::= "{" ws "\"s\"" ws ":" ws number ws "," ws "\"t\"" ws ":" ws number ws "," ws "\"u\"" ws ":" ws number ws "," ws "\"r\"" ws ":" ws number ws "," ws "\"a\"" ws ":" ws array ws "}"
array ::= "[" ws (number (ws "," ws number)*)? ws "]"
number ::= [0-9]+
ws ::= [ \t\n\r]*
